link.description:
How Open Nexus actually works — and how we prove it does.

A minimal RISC-V microkernel, thin service daemons over safe userspace libraries, and capability-based security enforced at the architecture level. This is a curated entry point; the canonical, line-level reference lives in the OS repository.

Suggested order: Overview → The NEURON kernel → Services & userspace → How we prove it → Codebase map → Research & papers.
